mission.title: kubeflow: Support theia or microsoft visual studio code
mission.description: Support theia or microsoft visual studio code. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubeflow deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubeflow version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
helm list -n kubeflow 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubeflow installation.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
It is often useful to have a proper IDE when developing models. For example, even when using Jupyter, users may choose to organize python code into modules. They might also want to create additional files like YAML files.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support theia or microsoft visual studio code
steps.description:
### Steps to build code-server on kubeflow:

1. Install `code-server`: `curl -fsSL https://code-server.dev/install.sh | sh`
1. Install `jupyter-server-proxy` : `pip3 install jupyter-server-proxy`
1. Install `jupyter-vscode-proxy`: `pip3 install jupyter-vscode-proxy`
1. Install `server-proxy` extension: `jupyter labextension install /server-proxy` or use extensions manager on jupyterlab frontend.

### Steps to build other apps on kubeflow

1. Install your app
2. Install `jupyter-server-proxy` :

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support theia or microsoft visual studio code" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Steps to build code-server on kubeflow:

1. Install `code-server`: `curl -fsSL https://code-server.dev/install.sh | sh`
1. Install `jupyter-server-proxy` : `pip3 install jupyter-server-proxy`
1. Install `jupyter-vscode-proxy`: `pip3 install jupyter-vscode-proxy`
1. Install `server-proxy` extension: `jupyter labextension install /server-proxy` or use extensions manager on jupyterlab frontend.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
